stocks.recent_news: Stock ~Rs 1,389 close April 7, 2026; gapped up 5.81% on April 8 on specialty-chem sector strength. 52-week range Rs 1,280-2,174 (stock ~36% below 52-week high). Q3 FY26 net profit Rs 99.79 cr (+1.73% YoY, -15.94% QoQ); sales Rs 1,974.97 cr (+3.76% YoY). Confirmed on April 17 it is not a Large Corporate under SEBI debt norms. Subsidiary Deepak Phenolics received adverse GST appellate order upheld on April 1, 2026 (~Rs 4.30 cr combined liability). Next earnings expected May 30, 2026.
stocks.sector_context: Indian specialty chemicals market US$64.5B (2024) projected to US$92.6B by 2033; domestic specialty chem forecast ~11% CAGR over 5 years. India-UK FTA (July 2025) removes tariffs on 99% of Indian exports - chemicals a key beneficiary. Offsetting positive: global PC resin market is oversupplied with weak downstream demand in autos/electronics/construction; ~5% of global PC capacity has shut. China+1 thematic still supportive long-term.
stocks.peer_comparison: Trading at P/E ~37.9 vs industry average ~24.7 - rich premium. Peers: SRF, Aarti Industries, Navin Fluorine, PI Industries. Prabhudas Lilladher target Rs 1,354 (REDUCE) below CMP. Deepak's commodity-heavy phenolics mix lags fluorine/agrochem peers whose end-markets are more resilient.
red_flags: Prabhudas Lilladher downgrade to REDUCE with target Rs 1,354 (near/below CMP)
Rs 85 billion PC resin capex entering a globally oversupplied market with declining prices
P/E 37.9 vs industry 24.7 - valuation premium unsupported by earnings momentum
Q3 FY26 PAT -15.94% QoQ - sequential margin pressure visible
GST appellate order (April 1, 2026) against subsidiary Deepak Phenolics - ~Rs 4.3 cr combined
Stock ~36% off 52-week high of Rs 2,174 indicates persistent de-rating
catalysts: Q4 FY26 results and final dividend expected around May 30, 2026
Phenol/acetone spread normalisation and agrochem intermediate approvals
India-UK FTA tariff benefits on chemical exports
Dahej capacity ramp-up through FY27

stocks.recent_news: Board meeting TODAY (April 23, 2026) to approve Q4 FY26 and FY26 audited results; earnings concall scheduled April 24, 2026 at 11:00 AM IST. Commissioned 1,000 MW Kudus-Aarey HVDC link for Mumbai on April 14, 2026. Crossed 1 crore cumulative smart meters; order book 2.46 cr meters worth ~Rs 29,519 cr. CARE ESG assigned first ESG rating 86.8/100 (CareEdge-ESG 1+) on April 15, 2026. Stock up ~37% over past year. Q3 FY26 PAT was Rs 574 cr (-8.2% YoY) despite revenue +15.73% - margin concerns pending Q4.
stocks.sector_context: Indian power T&D capex pipeline ~Rs 9 trillion (US$96.7B) through 2032 - multi-year structural tailwind. Near-term slowdown in FY26 ordering (16 schemes vs 45 in FY25) due to execution/capacity constraints, not demand. Renewable integration driving HVDC + smart-metering demand. Adani group tailwind from broader regulatory de-risking.
stocks.peer_comparison: P/E ~67 - premium vs Power Grid (~20) reflects smart-metering growth optionality. Smart-meter scale leadership vs Genus Power, HPL Electric. Mumbai distribution reliability benchmark-leading vs Tata Power. Q3 YoY PAT decline lagged Power Grid's steadier earnings.
red_flags: Binary event risk - Q4 FY26 results today + concall tomorrow; directional move likely
Q3 FY26 PAT -8.2% YoY signals margin compression despite revenue growth
P/E ~67 leaves little cushion if Q4 earnings miss consensus
Adani group regulatory overhang: SEC bribery case + pending SEBI matters
Capex-heavy model creates debt + interest rate sensitivity
catalysts: Q4 FY26 + FY26 audited results on April 23, 2026 (TODAY)
Q4 earnings concall on April 24, 2026 with FY27 guidance
Smart meter order book execution update (2.46 cr meters, Rs 29,519 cr)
Mumbai HVDC commissioning starts contributing to transmission revenue
Final dividend announcement

stocks.recent_news: Stock ~Rs 620 in early April 2026 vs 52-week range Rs 520-780 - trading closer to lows. Q4 FY26 board meeting + earnings concall scheduled April 29, 2026 at 5:00 PM IST (dividend to be considered). Chantilly, Virginia facility (US arm) underwent USFDA inspection March 30 - April 3, 2026, receiving 4 Form 483 observations - procedural only with no data integrity issues flagged. Analyst Q4 FY26 estimates: revenue Rs 1,280-1,380 cr, PAT Rs 170-195 cr, EBITDA margin 20-22%. Three-year profit growth -6.17%, revenue growth -2.19%.
stocks.sector_context: Indian pharma regulatory environment improving - USFDA OAI (most severe classification) cases at Indian facilities nearly halved in 2025 (11 in drugs vs 20 in 2024). India has 396 USFDA-approved facilities (more than US's 342). Indian pharma projected to reach US$70.3B by 2027 driven by exports. Overall sector tailwind contrasts with Granules company-specific legacy issues.
stocks.peer_comparison: Weak 3-year fundamentals vs peers like Divi's, Laurus Labs, Dr Reddy's. Stock near 52-week low while peers trade closer to highs indicates relative underperformance. API-heavy model less diversified than formulations peers who have benefited more from US generics momentum.
red_flags: 4 Form 483 observations at Chantilly VA facility (April 3, 2026) - second inspection within a year
Unresolved Gagillapur facility USFDA warning letter from February 2025 (contamination + data integrity)
Telangana plant warning letter March 2025 for GMP violations remains open
3-year profit growth negative (-6.17%) and revenue growth negative (-2.19%)
Stock trading ~20% above 52-week low and ~20% below 52-week high - persistent weakness
API concentration makes earnings volatile vs diversified formulations peers
catalysts: Q4 FY26 results + final dividend consideration on April 29, 2026
Potential resolution progress on Gagillapur/Telangana warning letters
Analyst PAT consensus Rs 170-195 cr - beat could trigger short covering
Improving broader USFDA environment for Indian pharma

stocks.recent_news: Stock Rs 981.20 close April 15, 2026; market cap Rs 63,569 cr (April 17). Q4 FY26 audited results scheduled April 27, 2026 with analyst/investor concall same day 6:00 PM IST - board to also consider dividend and renew fund-raising limits. Uttam Tibrewal completed Whole-Time Director tenure April 18, 2026 - continues as Deputy CEO (orderly succession). Q3 FY26 standalone PAT Rs 667.66 cr (+26.34% YoY vs Rs 528.45 cr). Analyst Q4 estimates: NIM 5.4-5.6%, PAT Rs 520-580 cr (below Q3 sequentially - normal seasonality).
stocks.sector_context: AU is the FIRST small finance bank to receive RBI in-principle approval (August 2025) to transition to a universal bank. March 6, 2026 RBI revised guidelines eased NOFHC requirement. Competitors rejected: Ujjivan SFB application returned 2026, Jana SFB returned October 2025. Structural moat for AU in SFB space. RBI sector focus: portfolio diversification and asset quality. AU MD/CEO Sanjay Agarwal received 3-year RBI extension Feb 2026.
stocks.peer_comparison: AU outperforms SFB peers on regulatory trajectory - only SFB with universal bank approval. Q3 PAT growth +26.34% YoY stronger than Ujjivan / Equitas / Jana. Transition to universal bank unlocks lower cost of funds and broader product suite - multi-year re-rating potential vs private banks (Kotak, IndusInd). FD rate 7.00-7.20% competitive.
red_flags: Binary event risk - Q4 FY26 results April 27 with guidance + dividend decision
Q4 PAT guidance sequentially lower than Q3 (Rs 520-580 cr vs Rs 667 cr)
Universal bank transition brings near-term compliance and capital requirements
Asset quality remains RBI focus area for SFB-to-universal transitions
catalysts: Q4 FY26 audited results + concall + dividend on April 27, 2026
Universal bank transition milestones - first SFB to make the jump
Fund-raising limit renewal at April 27 board meeting
Continuity under Sanjay Agarwal (3-year RBI-approved extension) and Tibrewal transition
Relaxed NOFHC rules (March 6, 2026) ease transition path

stocks.recent_news: CCI approved Torrent Power's 100% acquisition of Nabha Power from L&T Power Development on April 7, 2026 - enterprise value Rs 6,889 cr. Nabha operates 2x700 MW supercritical thermal plant at Rajpura, Punjab. Post-closure capacity rises from 5 GW to 6.4 GW. Long-term LNG SPA with JERA (Japan) - 4 cargoes/~270,000 tpy for 10 years starting 2027, DES basis. Met Kotak Institutional Equities in Ahmedabad on April 13, 2026. Q3 FY26 net profit Rs 643.08 cr (+35.2% YoY). Stock ~Rs 1,680 in April; market cap Rs 80,500 cr. Next earnings expected May 13, 2026.
stocks.sector_context: Indian power sector supported by ~Rs 9 trillion T&D capex through 2032, rising base load demand, and renewables push. Thermal + gas + renewable mix increasingly valued. Torrent's Ahmedabad/Surat distribution + Dahej gas plants provide integrated model vs peers.
stocks.peer_comparison: Q3 PAT growth +35.2% YoY stronger than NTPC (~10-12%) and matched by Adani Power. Analyst consensus target Rs 2,000-2,300 implies 19-37% upside from CMP. Nabha adds coal optionality vs increasingly renewable-tilted peers. JERA LNG deal is a differentiated long-term feedstock lock-in - unique among listed Indian IPPs.
red_flags: Large acquisition (Rs 6,889 cr) brings integration + leverage risk
Coal thermal expansion against ESG-led capital preferences
Pre-earnings event risk (results around May 13)
JERA LNG supply economics depend on global LNG price trajectory into 2027
catalysts: Q4 FY26 results expected May 13, 2026
CCI-cleared Nabha Power acquisition closing - capacity step-up 5 GW to 6.4 GW
JERA 10-year LNG SPA commencement in 2027 - long-term feedstock visibility
Analyst consensus target Rs 2,000-2,300 (19-37% upside)
Kotak Institutional Equities coverage interaction (April 13)
